mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and financing costs, which raises equity valuations and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the next month when the cut is part of an easing cycle rather than a one-off move.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, which should lift near-term earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns within one trading week after the release.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises month-over-month by at least 0.4%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered events are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the signal loses directional accuracy for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE paired with slowing PAYEMS growth signals labor-market deterioration, which weakens consumption and earnings expectations and should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months while PAYEMS growth is below 0.1% month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not outperform a UNRATE-only rule and produces positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in INDPRO combined with stable or falling CPIAUCSL reflects a benign supply-side expansion, which supports margins and real activity and should be associated with positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive when INDPRO rises over a 3-month window and CPIAUCSL inflation is flat or falling, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains very low, real policy rates stay deeply negative, sustaining liquidity support and risk-taking; this configuration should be bullish for S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rises above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered state is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of cases, or if higher inflation at low policy rates does not improve returns relative to low-inflation baselines.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST after a prior housing uptrend signals weaker residential demand and broader cyclical cooling, which should pressure equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls for two consecutive months after a 6-month rise
failure_condition: Reject if the post-uptrend decline in HOUST is not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if the conditional mean return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A strong PAYEMS gain together with a low UNRATE indicates robust labor income and spending capacity, which should support corporate revenues and lift S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-strength signal does not outperform either proxy alone and if 5-day forward returns are not positive in a majority of high-signal months.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising INDPRO despite higher FEDFUNDS shows resilient real activity under restrictive policy, which should be bearish for equities only if rates are still rising; otherwise the signal fades.
failure_condition: Reject if the signal does not turn negative specifically when FEDFUNDS is rising over a 3-month window, or if 60-day SPX returns remain positive across tightening episodes.
